senses.gloss: to unify, to unite, to reduce to one

examples.text: ووحَّد محمّدُ العربَ لأوّل مرّة على ديانة توحيدية ودولة موحّدة.
examples.english: Muhammad united the Arabs for the first time in a monotheistic religion and a unified state.

senses.gloss: to consider as single or unique
verbal noun of وَحَدَ (waḥada, “to be single, alone, unique, incomparable”) (form I)
verbal noun of وَحِدَ (waḥida, “to be single, alone, unique, incomparable”) (form I)

etymology.text:
Compare وَاحِد (wāḥid, “one”).

Causative of وَحَدَ (waḥada) and وَحِدَ (waḥida) and وَحُدَ (waḥuda).
